items.title: Reverse Sync: Harvest from Other Harnesses
items.description: Let users pull useful rules, conventions, or prompts they've written in Gemini/Codex/Cursor back into Claude Code as the canonical source. Solves the 'I accidentally wrote a great AGENTS.md rule and now it lives only in Codex' problem. One-way sync ignores the reality that users iterate in multiple tools.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Layer
items.description: Allow users to define harness-specific additions or suppressions on top of the synced base. E.g. 'always add this Cursor-specific rule, never sync this skill to Aider.' Solves the all-or-nothing problem where global sync breaks harness-specific workflows that have no equivalent elsewhere.

items.title: Sync Conflict Resolution Wizard
items.description: When a target harness has local edits that conflict with an incoming sync, present a structured diff and ask the user how to resolve: keep local, overwrite, or merge. Currently sync silently overwrites. Users who edit Cursor rules manually lose their work without warning.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Let users define named profiles (e.g. 'work', 'oss', 'fullstack') with different sync targets, section filters, and account mappings. Switch with /sync --profile work. Solves context-switching overhead for developers who maintain separate setups for different clients or project types.

items.title: Harness Compatibility Score
items.description: Before syncing, compute and display a compatibility score per target (e.g. 'Codex: 87% compatible, 3 features will be approximated, 1 dropped'). Shows users exactly what they're losing in translation before it happens, reducing surprise and frustration.

items.title: Starter Config Template Library
items.description: Curated CLAUDE.md + skills + MCP server bundles for common developer archetypes: fullstack web dev, data scientist, DevOps engineer, open source maintainer. Users install via /sync-setup --template fullstack and get a proven config synced everywhere immediately. Solves the cold-start problem.

items.title: Harness Format Change Notifier
items.description: Monitor upstream harness repos (Gemini CLI, Codex, Cursor) for config format changes and alert users when a new version breaks or changes sync compatibility. Right now users discover breakage only after syncing fails silently. Proactive notification prevents drift.

items.title: Git Branch-Aware Sync
items.description: Automatically switch which config is synced based on the active git branch. Experimental features on a feature branch can sync only to Cursor for testing, while main stays synced everywhere. Solves the problem of syncing WIP or experimental config to production harnesses.

items.title: Migrate INTO Claude Code Wizard
items.description: Guided flow to import an existing AGENTS.md, Cursor rules, or Aider CONVENTIONS.md into CLAUDE.md as the canonical source, then immediately sync everywhere. Lowers the barrier for users currently using another harness as their source of truth to switch to Claude Code.

items.title: Sync Impact Preview (Dry Run UI)
items.description: Before applying a sync, show a unified view of every file that would change across all targets: added lines, removed lines, new files, deleted files. Users can approve or veto per-target before anything touches disk. Reduces anxiety about destructive syncs.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: Let teams publish their CLAUDE.md + sync config to a shared git repo that teammates pull and sync locally. One team lead maintains the canonical config; everyone else runs /sync --from-team. Solves the team alignment problem where each developer has subtly different AI rules.

items.title: AI-Powered Concept Translation
items.description: When a Claude Code feature has no direct equivalent in a target harness, use Claude to intelligently approximate it rather than dropping it silently. E.g. translate a Claude Code skill into a Gemini command with equivalent behavior. Maximizes fidelity across harnesses.

items.title: Safe Secrets & Env Var Sync
items.description: Detect API keys, tokens, and env var references in config files before syncing and either redact them, substitute with harness-native env var syntax, or warn the user. Prevents accidental secret exposure when syncing to files that might be committed to git.

items.title: Auto-Discover Newly Installed Harnesses
items.description: Detect when a user installs a new AI coding tool (Cursor, Aider, Windsurf, etc.) and proactively offer to configure it. Background scan on SessionStart or daily cron checks for known harness binaries/dirs. Removes the manual step of registering new tools.

items.title: Sync History Timeline
items.description: Visual timeline of every sync event: what changed, which targets were updated, any failures or warnings. Filter by target, date range, or file. Solves the 'I don't know what state my Codex config is in or when it was last updated' problem.

items.title: Compliance-Pinned Rules
items.description: Mark specific rules or sections as compliance-critical so they are always synced, cannot be skipped by --skip-sections, and appear first in every target. For teams with security or legal requirements (e.g. 'never commit secrets', 'always ask before deleting'). Gives compliance teams confidence.

items.title: Cross-Harness Task Comparison
items.description: Run the same prompt or task description across multiple configured harnesses and display the responses side by side. Helps users understand behavioral differences caused by config translation gaps, and validates that a sync actually produced equivalent behavior.

items.title: Plugin & Skill Compatibility Matrix
items.description: For each installed Claude Code plugin/skill, show which harnesses support syncing it and what fidelity to expect. Interactive table view via /sync-matrix. Helps users understand their plugin investment and which harnesses fully benefit from their setup.

items.title: Config Health Score Dashboard
items.description: Overall health metric (0-100) for each synced harness combining: recency of last sync, number of dropped features, config errors, parity gaps, and unreachable MCP servers. Single glance tells you which harnesses are degraded without reading logs.

items.title: Interactive First-Run Onboarding Wizard
items.description: Guided setup flow for new users that detects installed harnesses, walks through which to enable, configures accounts, runs first sync, and explains what was translated vs. dropped. Replaces the current read-the-README experience with a conversational, safe introduction.

items.title: Sync Notifications via Slack/Discord
items.description: Send a notification to a configured Slack or Discord webhook when a sync completes, fails, or detects drift. Useful for teams where one person manages the shared config and others need to know when it updates. Also useful for personal setups monitoring unattended scheduled syncs.

items.title: Config Inheritance Hierarchy
items.description: Support a base global config + per-project overrides pattern, mirroring how many tool configs work. A root CLAUDE.md defines standards, project-level CLAUDE.md adds project-specific rules, and both get synced with the project context understood. Solves the global-vs-project tension.

items.title: Shareable Config Bundle Export
items.description: Export a sanitized, portable .harness-bundle.zip containing all rules, skills, and MCP server definitions (with secrets stripped) that another developer can import. Enables config sharing on GitHub, blog posts, or team wikis as a first-class feature.

items.title: MCP Server Reachability Alerts
items.description: Continuously monitor configured MCP servers for reachability and alert when one goes down or changes URL. Surface this in /sync-health and optionally via desktop notification. Solves the silent failure mode where tools appear configured but are actually broken.

items.title: Natural Language Rule Authoring
items.description: Let users describe a rule they want in plain English ('always write tests before implementation code') and have Claude generate the appropriate CLAUDE.md section, then sync it everywhere. Lowers the barrier for users who know what behavior they want but don't know the config syntax.

items.title: Config Version Pinning Per Target
items.description: Pin a specific sync snapshot version to a target harness (e.g. keep Aider on the config from last week while testing new rules in Cursor). Useful for stable/experimental config channels. Users can validate new config in one harness before promoting everywhere.

items.title: Audit Log Export for Compliance
items.description: Export complete sync history as structured JSON or CSV: who triggered, what changed, which targets, timestamps, outcomes. Supports SOC2/compliance requirements for teams that need evidence of config governance. Also useful for debugging long-standing drift issues.

items.title: CI/CD Sync Integration
items.description: GitHub Action / GitLab CI step that runs HarnessSync in a pipeline, ensuring team config is always synced when CLAUDE.md changes are merged. Treats config drift as a CI failure. Solves the 'someone updated CLAUDE.md but nobody re-synced' problem on teams.

items.title: Parity Gap Explainer
items.description: For every feature that cannot be synced to a target harness, provide a plain-English explanation of why and what the closest approximation is. Instead of silently dropping, say 'Gemini CLI has no skill concept; your skill was converted to a command with the same prompt.' Builds user trust.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track which synced harnesses are actually being invoked (via process detection or file access timestamps) and surface which ones have gone stale. Tell the user 'You haven't used Aider in 30 days — remove it from sync to reduce noise?' Keeps the sync target list clean.

items.title: Interactive Section Toggle UI
items.description: Visual TUI for toggling which CLAUDE.md sections sync to which targets. Checkbox-style interface via /sync-map that's more ergonomic than editing YAML. Solves the UX friction of managing only_sections/skip_sections per adapter via config files.

items.title: Auto-Sync on PR Merge
items.description: Git hook integration that triggers a full sync whenever a PR touching CLAUDE.md, .claude/skills/, or .mcp.json is merged. Ensures config changes propagate immediately without manual /sync invocation. Critical for teams using PRs to govern AI config changes.

items.title: MCP Server Cost Visibility
items.description: For synced MCP servers that call paid APIs, surface estimated token/request costs per harness in /sync-health. Help users understand the cost implications of having the same expensive MCP server configured in 5 harnesses simultaneously.

items.title: Config Quality Linter with Suggestions
items.description: Beyond just finding errors, /sync-lint should suggest improvements: 'Your CLAUDE.md has no section headers — adding them enables selective sync', 'This MCP server URL is hardcoded — use an env var for portability.' Educates users while enforcing quality.

items.title: Desktop Notifications for Sync Events
items.description: Native macOS/Linux desktop notification when auto-sync triggers, completes, or fails. Users currently have no signal that a PostToolUse hook fired and synced (or failed to sync). Passive awareness without requiring a terminal window.

items.title: Harness-Specific Tuning Advisor
items.description: After syncing, analyze the translated config and suggest harness-native optimizations: 'Cursor MDC rules support priority weights — add these to get better ordering', 'Gemini supports agent-level tool restrictions not present in your sync.' Maximizes value in each harness.

items.title: Sync Sandbox / Preview Environment
items.description: Sync to a temporary isolated directory instead of the real harness config location, letting users inspect results before committing. /sync --sandbox creates a harness-sync-preview/ folder with exactly what would be written. Zero-risk exploration of a new sync config.

items.title: Auto-Generated Config Changelog
items.description: Automatically generate a human-readable changelog entry every time config changes are synced: 'Added 2 new skills, updated MCP server URL for fetch, removed deprecated rule about console.log.' Helps users track their AI config evolution over time like they track code.

items.title: Harness Benchmark Report
items.description: Run a standard set of prompts across all configured harnesses and score them on speed, quality, and instruction-following fidelity relative to Claude Code. Gives users data-driven insight into which harnesses best replicate their Claude Code experience after sync.

items.title: Community Config Registry
items.description: Public registry where users publish and discover HarnessSync config bundles by category (e.g. 'TypeScript expert', 'security auditor', 'technical writer'). Browse, preview, and install with one command. Creates network effects and a learning resource for new users.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
